title: The 0615bc5 missing-await bug EXISTS AGAIN at a hotter call site: _compose_section_per_basket is a sync def called with no await/to_thread from the async _run_section — it blocks the event loop for the ENTIRE per-basket compose of a section
mechanism: `_compose_section_per_basket` is defined as a plain `def` (verified_compose.py:3262), not `async def`. `_run_section` (multi_section_generator.py:5182) is `async def`. At :5468 it is called as `_vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(...)` — no `await`, no `asyncio.to_thread`. This function is the MAIN per-basket compose loop: it walks every basket in the section, runs writer_fn + verify_fn + the bounded repair loop (redraft_fn) for each. All of it executes synchronously ON the asyncio event loop. Consequence: while section A is inside it, the single event loop is pinned — section B's coroutine cannot resume, and no in-flight writer/judge HTTP response can be processed. PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 is therefore fiction: the two 'parallel' sections are serialized. The same file already uses `await asyncio.to_thread(strict_verify, ...)` at :5667 and :10288, so the authors knew the pattern — :5468 and :5514 were simply missed, exactly as _draft_passes_wrapper was.
fix: Wrap both call sites: `_vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(functools.partial(_compose_section_per_basket, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool, writer_fn=..., verify_fn=..., redraft_fn=..., ...))`. This is the identical remedy applied in 0615bc5. Note it must be done TOGETHER with the redraft fix below, otherwise the offloaded thread still spins a fresh loop per redraft.

title: _redraft spins a FRESH asyncio event loop per call, which makes get_semaphore() destroy and recreate the module-global LLM concurrency semaphore — 303 times in one run. PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM is not enforced at all.
mechanism: `_redraft` (the group re-draft closure fed into _compose_one_basket's bounded repair loop) is a sync function. At :4993-4994 it creates a new `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)` and does `fut = _ex.submit(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))` — a BRAND NEW event loop for every single re-draft. Inside `_run()`, `_call_writer` -> `OpenRouterClient._call_api` (openrouter_client.py:1890) calls `get_semaphore()`. get_semaphore (llm_provider.py:149) guards on `_LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop` and, on mismatch, REBINDS the module-global `_LLM_SEMAPHORE = asyncio.Semaphore(cap)`. Because the redraft loop is never the main compose loop, every alternation main-loop -> redraft-loop -> main-loop destroys the semaphore TWICE. Permits held by calls already in flight on the old object simply vanish; the fresh semaphore starts at full cap. So the global cap of 30 bounds nothing, and burst concurrency is unbounded — which is what produces the 429/connection-error storm. Separately, `fut.result(timeout=call_deadline_s + 30.0)` at :4996 is a BLOCKING wait of up to 330s, and because of finding 1 it executes on the event loop.
fix: Make _redraft async (`async def _redraft`) and await `_call_writer` directly on the caller's loop — the ThreadPoolExecutor+asyncio.run sandwich exists only because the caller chain is sync. If it must stay sync, pass the running loop in and use `asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(_run(), main_loop)` instead of `asyncio.run(_run())`. Independently, harden llm_provider.get_semaphore: key the semaphore in a `WeakKeyDictionary[loop -> Semaphore]` instead of clobbering one module global, so a second loop cannot destroy the first loop's cap.

title: Every NLI judge POST is gated by a process-global BoundedSemaphore(4) — acquired ON the event loop thread
mechanism: `_post_with_total_deadline` opens with `with _acquire_judge_slot():` BEFORE submitting the POST. `acquire_judge_slot` resolves PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY, which is set in NO launch script, so it falls to DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4, and does a blocking `sem.acquire()` on a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore. Two consequences: (a) at most 4 entailment POSTs are in flight process-wide no matter that PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12 and PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 — the fixed pre-pass's 12 verify threads immediately queue 8-deep behind a cap of 4, so the 0615bc5 speedup is itself capped; (b) on the compose leg (finding 1) the thread doing sem.acquire() IS the event loop, so slot-queueing time is loop-freeze time on top of POST time.
fix: Set PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY explicitly in the launch script (it was sized at 4 for a 4-host mirror chain during a 429 storm; with the compose leg off-loop, raise it to match PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM and watch 429 rate). Independently: never call acquire_judge_slot from the loop thread.

title: time.sleep() rate-limit backoff (15-60s) inside the judge retry runs on the event loop thread
mechanism: `_EntailmentJudge.judge()` (:740) is fully synchronous. On a retryable fault it computes `_sleep_s = _rate_limit_backoff_s(...)` and calls `time.sleep(_sleep_s)` — not asyncio.sleep. For a 429/503/DNS fault the backoff is floored at PG_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_FLOOR_S=15.0 and capped at PG_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_CAP_S=60.0, with a LARGER retry budget than the ordinary path. Reached from the compose leg (finding 1), that time.sleep executes on the asyncio event loop: one 429 on one sentence freezes the ENTIRE report generation — all sections, all pre-pass writer calls — for 15-60s, repeatedly. The module's own docstring says 429 storms on the mirror-chain lead host are the expected steady state.
fix: Getting the compose leg off-loop (finding 1) makes these sleeps block a worker thread, which is correct. Do not leave any path where judge() can be entered from the loop thread.

title: _compose_section_per_basket is a SYNC blocking basket loop called with no await/to_thread from the async _run_section - it serializes every per-basket NLI judge AND freezes the whole event loop (all other sections)
mechanism: _run_section is `async def`. At :5468 it calls `_vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(...)` as a plain function call. inspect.iscoroutinefunction(_compose_section_per_basket) == False. Its body loops `for basket in (section_baskets or [])` (verified_compose.py:3325) and, per basket, calls _compose_one_basket / compose_basket_multicited_synth_primary / compose_companion_figure_units (nested member x sentence-unit loop at :3414), each of which calls the SYNC verify_fn(sentence, scoped_pool) (verified_compose.py:2222, 2583, 2981, 3071, 3192) -> make_writer_verify_fn -> verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2154, sync) -> entailment_judge.judge() (sync) -> _post_with_total_deadline -> fut.result(timeout) which BLOCKS THE CALLING THREAD. The calling thread is the asyncio event loop. So (a) all per-basket verification in a section runs one judge POST at a time, and (b) for the entire duration the event loop is frozen, so every OTHER section's writer tasks, timeouts and HTTP callbacks make ZERO progress. This is exactly the 0615bc5 bug class, one level up, unfixed.
fix: Wrap both call sites in `await asyncio.to_thread(_compose_section_per_basket, ...)` (unblocks the loop immediately, same as 0615bc5), then go further: make the per-basket loop fan out - either an async _compose_section_per_basket that gathers per-basket coroutines under a semaphore, or a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over baskets with a copied contextvars context (the pattern already written for strict_verify at provenance_generator.py:3676-3718). Baskets are independent; verdicts are order-independent and are reassembled by index.

title: The post-draft tail is ~85-88% of every section's wall clock and has NO fan-out at all - the parallel writer pre-pass is only ~12-15% of the work
mechanism: The execution graph per section is: parallel pre-pass (:5448, Semaphore(24)) -> then a strictly serial chain of five blocking/serial stages: _compose_section_per_basket (:5468), _repair_untokened_draft (:5575), _coherence_rewrite (:5591), strict_verify (:5667), sentence repair (:5693), optional regen + a SECOND strict_verify (:5775/:5804). None of these stages pipelines with the next, and none of them fans out over sentences/baskets. So raising PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY / PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM can only ever compress the 12-15% slice; the other 85% is immune to every concurrency knob in the launch scripts.
fix: Parallelize inside the tail (see the other findings: basket loop, strict_verify workers, coherence re-verify, sentence repair) and/or pipeline it: start the next section's pre-pass while the previous section's verify tail runs - which becomes automatic once the tail stops blocking the event loop.

title: 94% of the baskets live in ONE section coroutine ('Additional Corroborated Findings', 304-326 of 346), and it is scheduled LAST behind a Semaphore(2)
mechanism: plans=4 -> 5 sections. Sections 0-3 carry 15-18 baskets each; section 4 carries 304-326. sec_sema = Semaphore(PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS) = 2 (iter4/iter5 launchers) and tasks are created in plan order, so section 4 only acquires the semaphore after two of the four small sections complete - i.e. it STARTS ~50-90 minutes into the run. Because the section is the unit of parallelism and its whole compose+verify tail is the single-threaded loop of finding #1, 94% of the work has an achieved parallelism of exactly 1. PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS cannot help: there is no second big section to overlap with.
fix: Two independent fixes: (1) shard the mega-section's baskets into N chunks composed by N concurrent tasks (the basket loop is already order-stable and dedup is done on the concatenated list, so chunking + in-order reassembly is safe); (2) schedule sections longest-first (sort tasks by len(section.ev_ids) descending) so the mega-section starts at t=0 and overlaps with all the small ones instead of being tail-scheduled.

title: strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop runs SERIAL because PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is never set - the bounded thread pool exists and is dead code in production
mechanism: _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset/empty (:3390-3392). strict_verify then takes the serial branch at :3666-3676 ('if _verify_workers < 2 ... byte-identical to the legacy loop'), running one blocking entailment-judge POST per sentence, sequentially. The ThreadPoolExecutor path at :3678-3718 - which already handles contextvars copying and cost-ledger reconciliation - is never entered. strict_verify is invoked twice per section (:5667 and, on regen, :5804). grep shows PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY appears in NO launch_compose_gear_*.sh and in no os.environ.setdefault in run_s5_i3.py (which does setdefault 5 other flags at :342-359).
fix: export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8 (or setdefault it in run_s5_i3.py next to the other Fix-3/Fix-6 setdefaults at :342-359). Zero code change; the parallel path is already written, tested and cost-reconciled.

title: coherence_rewrite_section is an async coroutine that calls the SYNC blocking verify_sentence_provenance once per sentence in a for-loop - blocking the event loop again
mechanism: `async def coherence_rewrite_section` is awaited from _run_section at multi_section_generator.py:5591. After the single rewrite LLM call it does `for orig, new in zip(sentences, new_sentences):` (:271) and inside calls `res = verify_sentence_provenance(new, evidence_pool)` (:283) - the SYNC verifier, which runs the entailment judge over a blocking fut.result(). No await, no to_thread. So the whole re-verify sweep is serial AND runs on the event loop thread, freezing every other section for N_sentences x ~5s. Default-ON (PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS kill-switch defaults on via _flag_on(default_on=True)).
fix: Hoist the whole re-verify loop into one `await asyncio.to_thread(...)` call, and inside it use a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over the changed sentences (only the sentences where new != orig and the token multiset matches actually need a judge call).

title: The authoritative NLI verify gate is a fully SERIAL sync loop — judge concurrency ceiling is PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS (=2), not 12/16/48. This is what the await fix did NOT fix.
mechanism: verified_compose.py:3325 `for basket in (section_baskets or []):` is a plain serial for-loop inside a plain sync `def _compose_section_per_basket`. Each iteration calls _compose_one_basket (:2192), which at :2221-2222 runs `for sentence in split_into_sentences(draft): res = verify_fn(sentence, scoped_pool)` — another plain serial for-loop. verify_fn -> verify_sentence_provenance -> provenance_generator.py:2558 `_get_judge().judge(...)` -> a BLOCKING NLI POST (mean 8.9s). The ENTIRE verified_compose.py module contains zero asyncio, zero ThreadPoolExecutor, zero `async def` (grep count = 0). So every sentence that actually ships is NLI-judged one-at-a-time, basket-after-basket. The only concurrency above this is scripts/run_s5_i3.py:491 `asyncio.Semaphore(PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2)` — 2 sections. The judge's hard concurrency ceiling is therefore 2, no matter what PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12 / PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 say. The abstractive_writer async pre-pass (now correctly off-loop) only PRECOMPUTES drafts — abstractive_writer.py:1113 states the sync writer_fn _compose_one_basket reads is 'a pure dict lookup'. Parallelizing the writer while leaving the verifier serial parallelizes 30% of the calls and leaves 70% single-file.
fix: Parallelize the two loops. (a) In _compose_one_basket (:2221), verify the draft's sentences through a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor with a copied contextvars context — the pattern already exists and is battle-tested at provenance_generator.py:3716 (_verify_in_context / _parent_ctx.copy().run). (b) Run _compose_section_per_basket's basket loop (:3325) over a bounded pool too, or hoist it into the writer's existing async gather. (c) Raise PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS. Note verdicts are order-independent and results are reassembled by index, exactly as provenance_generator's parallel path already does.

title: Process-global side-judge semaphore hard-caps ALL NLI POSTs at 4 in flight — never set in any launch script, and will become the binding cap the moment finding #1 is fixed
mechanism: judge_concurrency.py:55 `DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4`. entailment_judge.py:155 wraps EVERY judge POST in `with _acquire_judge_slot():`, which acquires one slot of a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore sized by resolve_max_concurrency() (:81). PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY is not set in launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh, iter4, iter3, or scripts/run_s5_i3.py -> resolves to 4. The semaphore is SHARED with credibility_judge_caller and semantic_conflict_detector, so those consume compose's judge slots too. This cap was tuned for a 20-way credibility burst against a 4-host mirror chain ([friendli, novita, z-ai, phala]) — it is not a compose-throughput number. The credibility pass gets a phase override (multi_section_generator.py:9527, credibility_pass_concurrency) that raises its cap; the compose-time entailment verify has NO override, so it runs at 4 forever. Consequence: even if you parallelize the serial verify loop to the writer's 12, the judge still admits only 4 POSTs at a time.
fix: export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16 (or 24) in launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh. The mirror chain is 4 hosts with burst-spread ON (judge_burst_spread default 'spread'), so 16 in flight = ~4 POSTs/host — well within provider capacity and far below the ~32-simultaneous burst that originally 429-stormed friendli. Alternatively set PG_JUDGE_BURST_SPREAD=lb (judge_burst_spread.py:22) to drop the single-host pin and let OpenRouter load-balance across all glm-5.2 endpoints — the config file itself documents this as the D8-Judge cure.

title: A hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot (25% of global judge capacity) for 300s — and PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES is never wired, so one sentence can burn 900s of slot-time
mechanism: _post_with_total_deadline (:139) does `with _acquire_judge_slot():` at :155 and then blocks on `fut.result(timeout=total_s)` at :172 — the semaphore slot is held for the ENTIRE total deadline, not just for productive work. launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh:38 and run_s5_i3.py:357 both set PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300, DOUBLE the 150s code default (:136). With the cap of 4 (finding #2), ONE trickle-hung POST removes 25% of the entire pipeline's verify capacity for 5 minutes. Worse: _total_deadline_retries (:953) is `min(_retries, PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES)` and PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES is unset everywhere, so it defaults to _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES = 2 (:200) -> up to 3 attempts x 300s = 900s of slot-holding for a SINGLE sentence before it fail-closed drops. The code comment at :195-197 explicitly says 'the run slate sets PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1 to apply the fix' — grep shows NO launch script or run slate sets it. The A2 fix is un-wired in production.
fix: Set PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1 in launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh (the value the code comment says the slate is supposed to carry), and drop PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S back toward 120-150s — measured p90 is 13-14s and p50 is 5.4s, so a 300s wall is 20x the p90 and buys almost nothing but slot-blocking. Independently: release the semaphore slot before the retry backoff sleeps (it already is) AND consider not holding it across the full fut.result deadline for a call already known to be trickling.

title: Judge is called ONE (span, sentence) pair per HTTP POST — no batching — even where the caller already holds the whole sentence list
mechanism: entailment_judge.py:830-841 builds json_body with exactly one user message containing one SPAN and one SENTENCE (_ENTAILMENT_PROMPT at :584 is single-pair by construction). Every rendered sentence therefore costs one full OpenRouter round trip with reasoning effort='high' (:270). The natural batch site already exists: abstractive_writer.py:683-684 `for sentence in sentences: res = writer_verify_fn(sentence, scoped_pool)` — _draft_passes_wrapper has the ENTIRE draft's sentence list in hand and issues them one at a time; same for verified_compose.py:2221. The per-call payload is tiny relative to the latency: mean input 1008 tokens, mean output 245 tokens, against max_tokens=131072 (:267-268). So ~1k tokens of work is paying a full 8.9s (compose) / 19.7s (DR) TTFT+queue+reasoning-warmup round trip. Packing N pairs into one prompt and returning a JSON array of N verdicts is fully supported by the chat-completions API, keeps temperature=0.0 idempotency, and cuts POST count (and thus semaphore pressure from finding #2) by N.
fix: Add a `judge_batch(pairs: list[tuple[str,str]]) -> list[tuple[str,str]]` to _EntailmentJudge: one prompt enumerating N SPAN/SENTENCE pairs, returning a JSON array of N verdict objects; reuse _extract_first_json_object's fail-closed discipline per element and fall back to per-pair calls if the array length mismatches (never silently pad — that would fail OPEN). Wire it at abstractive_writer.py:683 and verified_compose.py:2221, both of which already have the full sentence list. Keep the existing verdict cache in front of it (check cache for each pair, batch only the misses).

title: The SHA-gated section RESUME never fires: a root daemon (gear_sync.sh) overwrites the compose's PINNED cp3 every ~96 min, so every relaunch declares all finished sections stale and recomposes them. Resume hit rate = 0/5 attempts.
mechanism: run_s5_i3.py:506-523 hashes cp2/cp3/cp4, compares to ckpt/inputs_sha.json, and on ANY mismatch sets `_stale=True` -> skips loading every `section_*_draft.json` -> recomposes all 5 sections from scratch. Meanwhile /workspace/gear_sync.sh runs an infinite `while true; sleep 20` loop as root (PID 575438, up since Jul 10 22:08) whose `mirror` function does `cp -f $src $SH/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json` whenever the S2/S3 gear emits a newer basket snapshot. That destination file is EXACTLY the path both launch_compose_gear_iter4.sh and iter5 pin as CP3. So the compose's 'pinned' input mutates underneath the running compose. Mean interval between cp3 promotions = 96 min; a single compose attempt needs 3h+. Input MTBF << attempt duration, so the resume is guaranteed to be invalidated before it can ever be used. This is a livelock, not a slowdown: forward progress is structurally zero.
fix: Two independent fixes, both needed. (a) The launcher must COPY cp2/cp3/cp4 into the run's own output dir at attempt-1 start and pass the copies, so a running compose has immutable inputs (snapshot-on-start). The 'gear rule: newest inputs win' can still apply BETWEEN runs, not DURING one. (b) run_s5_i3.py should key checkpoints per-input-SHA (e.g. ckpt/<sha12>/section_N_draft.json) so a changed input starts a new checkpoint namespace instead of silently discarding a valid one, and old namespaces remain resumable if the input reverts.

title: 94% of all baskets (326 of 346) are dumped into a single residual section, and sections run under Semaphore(2) — so the run's makespan is one serial section that needs 11-23h. cp5 is unreachable at --cap-primary 0.
mechanism: With `--cap-primary 0` (the DEFAULT, and what the gear launchers pass for a 'full-coverage acceptance run'), the section/basket map assigns 15-18 baskets each to sections 0-3 and sweeps everything else into a residual SectionPlan idx=4 — 326 of the 346 baskets. Sections are composed under `sec_sema = asyncio.Semaphore(PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS)` = 2 (run_s5_i3.py:491), and each section is one coroutine, so section 4 is a single indivisible serial unit. There is no intra-section fan-out across sections. The makespan of the whole compose is therefore set by section 4 alone, and cp5 is only written after ALL sections return. Compose parallelism is capped at 2 while 94% of the work sits in 1 unit — the 48-way parallelism the operator asked about cannot exist by construction.
fix: Shard the residual section: split its 326 baskets into ceil(326/K) sub-sections of ~16 baskets each and let them run under the same section semaphore (raise PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS accordingly), then concatenate. Basket rendering inside a section is already independent, so this is a pure fan-out. Alternatively, checkpoint at BASKET granularity inside a section rather than only at section completion, so section 4's 95 min of work is not thrown away on every kill.

title: The entailment judge builds a brand-new ThreadPoolExecutor + OS thread for EVERY judge POST, and the OpenRouter client is re-instantiated 1405 times for 3693 POSTs
mechanism: `_post_with_total_deadline` (entailment_judge.py:139) does `ex = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)` then `fut = ex.submit(client.post, ...)` then `ex.shutdown()` — one executor + one OS thread created and torn down per NLI judge POST, purely to get a `fut.result(timeout=total_s)` wall. Every rendered sentence is judged, so this is per-sentence thread churn. Separately, `OpenRouter client initialized` appears 1405 times for 3693 POSTs, i.e. a client (and its httpx connection pool) is rebuilt roughly every 2.6 requests — connection pooling and TLS session reuse are largely defeated, which is the most likely proximate cause of the 562 `Connection error, retrying` warnings rather than genuine network faults.
fix: Hoist a single module-level ThreadPoolExecutor (or use `httpx` async + `asyncio.wait_for`, which needs no thread at all) instead of one executor per POST. Hoist the OpenRouterClient to one long-lived instance per process (or per section) so the httpx connection pool and TLS sessions are actually reused across the 3693 POSTs.

title: The AUTHORITATIVE compose loop is a sync def called with no await from an async coroutine — the whole per-basket verify+redraft phase runs ON the event loop, concurrency 1. 0615bc5 fixed the pre-pass; this is the same bug one layer up and it is still at HEAD.
mechanism: `async def _run_section` (:5182) awaits `abstractive_pre_pass` (:5448 — now correctly parallel) and then calls `_vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(...)` at :5468 with NO await and NO asyncio.to_thread. `_compose_section_per_basket` is a plain `def` (verified_compose.py:3262). Inside it, per basket: (a) `verify_fn(sentence, pool)` = verify_sentence_provenance -> _get_judge().judge() (provenance_generator.py:2558) -> _post_with_total_deadline -> `fut.result(timeout=...)`, which BLOCKS THE CALLING THREAD; (b) `redraft_fn(...)` (verified_compose.py:2136) = the SYNTH_PRIMARY group re-draft, whose body does `fut = _ex.submit(lambda: asyncio.run(_run())); fut.result(timeout=call_deadline_s + 30.0)` (multi_section_generator.py:4994-4996) — a blocking wait of up to 330s on a FULL writer LLM call. The calling thread is the asyncio event loop. So for the entire compose phase of a section, the loop is frozen: no other section's pre-pass tasks run, no other basket's writer call runs, and even the judge's own 4-slot semaphore is irrelevant because only one judge call is ever issued at a time. The same defect exists on the non-abstractive branch at :5514. Commit 0615bc5 touched ONLY abstractive_writer.py (`git show --name-only`), so this path is untouched.
fix: `_vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(_compose_section_per_basket, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool, writer_fn=..., verify_fn=..., ...)` at :5468 and :5514 (functools.partial for the kwargs). That frees the loop so sibling sections' pre-passes keep drafting, and lets the judge's own semaphore (see next finding) actually admit 4+ concurrent POSTs instead of 1. Note _redraft's inner `asyncio.run` (:4994) already runs on its own worker thread, so it stays correct off-loop.

title: A stalled judge POST holds 1 of only 4 semaphore slots for the FULL 300s deadline — one hang removes 25% of process-wide verify capacity for 5 minutes
mechanism: The `with _acquire_judge_slot():` block at entailment_judge.py:155 wraps the whole POST INCLUDING `fut.result(timeout=total_s)` at :174 — the slot is held for the entire wall-deadline, not just for healthy calls. total_s = PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S, whose CODE default is 150s (entailment_judge.py:134) but which the compose launchers RAISE to 300s (launch_compose_gear_iter3.sh:36, iter4.sh:33, iter5.sh:38). Nobody raised the 4-slot pool to compensate. So the launcher doubled the worst-case slot-hold time against an unchanged 4-slot pool: each hung judge socket now sterilizes 25% of the entire process's verification throughput for a full 5 minutes. With the bounded retry (up to 3 attempts) a single bad claim can hold a quarter of all capacity for ~15 minutes.
fix: Two independent levers, both cheap: (a) raise PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY so one stall is a small fraction of the pool rather than 25% of it; (b) lower PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S back toward the measured p99 — live probe shows a healthy judge call is 4-5s, so a 300s wall is ~60x the real p99 and is pure dead slot-hold. A 30-60s wall would still be >6x headroom over the measured worst case while cutting the stall cost 5-10x.

title: PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM is neither global nor stable: it does not cover the NLI judge at all (~65% of all OpenRouter POSTs), and its semaphore is torn down and rebuilt on every event-loop switch — 306 times in a single run.
mechanism: Two independent leaks. (1) NOT GLOBAL: the semaphore is only acquired in `OpenRouterClient._call` (openrouter_client.py:1889-1890). The entailment judge does NOT use OpenRouterClient — it builds its own httpx.Client (entailment_judge.py:712 `_build_client`) and POSTs directly to `self._endpoint`. So every NLI judge call — the majority of the traffic — bypasses PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM entirely. In the iter4 run: 3699 total OpenRouter POSTs, of which only 1258 are writer generate() calls => ~2441 (66%) are judge POSTs, completely outside the 'global' cap. (2) NOT STABLE: get_semaphore() at :149 does `if _LLM_SEMAPHORE is None or _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop: _LLM_SEMAPHORE = asyncio.Semaphore(cap)` — it REPLACES the module-global whenever the running loop differs. `_redraft` (multi_section_generator.py:4994) submits `asyncio.run(_run())` to a worker thread, creating a BRAND NEW event loop per re-draft. Each redraft therefore forces a rebuild in the worker loop, and the main loop's next writer call forces a rebuild back — ping-pong. Every rebuild discards the in-flight count: tasks still holding the OLD semaphore object are invisible to the NEW one, which hands out `cap` fresh slots. The cap is not enforced across the alternation, which feeds the 429s the run then burns 15-50s backoffs on.
fix: Either (a) make the cap a threading-level bound shared by both transports (the judge's own bounded semaphore is already the right primitive — unify them), or (b) at minimum stop the per-loop rebind churn: cache the semaphore per-loop in a WeakKeyDictionary keyed by loop instead of replacing the single global. And stop creating a fresh event loop per re-draft — _redraft should reuse a persistent worker loop or be made async and awaited from an off-loop thread.

title: PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12 is LIVE but is silently undercut by PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 whenever two sections overlap (2 x 12 = 24 > 16), and is the ONLY lever on the 288-basket residual section that holds 83% of the corpus.
mechanism: `sem = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)` at :986 with concurrency read at :955 from PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY — genuinely LIVE, one basket task per basket, `async with semaphore` at :993, and `asyncio.wait(tasks, timeout=wall)` at :1055 (not a gather-of-1). But the semaphore is created PER abstractive_pre_pass CALL, i.e. per section, and run_s5_i3.py:491 runs 2 sections at once (`sec_sema = asyncio.Semaphore(int(os.environ.get('PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS', '2')))`, launcher exports 2). So up to 24 writer tasks contend for the 16-slot global LLM semaphore — the global cap wins, and the per-stage knob's headroom is dead. Worse, the basket distribution is pathological: the iter4 log shows sections of 18/15/15/17 ev_ids plus a residual section with 326 ev_ids (the writer logs 'basket-scaled wall: n=288 conc=24'). For most of the run only the residual is live, so PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS gives zero parallelism on 83% of the work and writer conc 12 is the entire budget for 288 baskets => 24 sequential waves, each gated on the 4-wide NLI judge.
fix: Raise PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM to >= sections x writer_conc (2 x 12 = 24, or just set it to 32) so the per-stage knob is the binding one, and split the residual section into several so PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS has something to parallelize. Confirm the split by watching for >1 concurrent '[section N] START' with N>=4.

title: PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000 is a DEAD env var — exported by all 9 launchers, read by ZERO lines of code; spans reach the writer unbounded and set the makespan tail
mechanism: Every launcher exports PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000, so operators believe span text entering the writer prompt is capped at 8000 chars. Nothing reads it. `grep -rn S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP` over src/ + scripts/ + config/ in BOTH trees returns 0 hits; 9 .sh files set it. _build_writer_prompt (:574) appends `f"SPAN {i}: {quote}"` with no truncation anywhere in the writer path. So the member's full direct_quote — which run_s5_i3.build_baskets sets to the ENTIRE evidence row text — goes in whole. Measured on the real corpus: row text median 2,527 chars, p90 11,435, max 24,915. Resulting writer prompts reach 11,389 in-tokens (from iter4's own log accounting). THROUGHPUT MECHANISM: in the uncontended regime latency scales steeply with basket size. From the iter3 log, median latency bucketed by input tokens: <2k -> 4.3s; 2-4k -> 12.4s; 4-6k -> 28.4s; 6-8k -> 28.8s; 8-10k -> 70.2s; 10-12k -> 98.5s. A 23x spread. These fat baskets are the TAIL that sets the wave makespan: _makespan_wall_seconds and the 300s PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S are both sized on averages, so a handful of 100-250s baskets stretches every wave and eats the wall. HONEST CAVEAT: in iter4 corr(IN,latency) = -0.001 because provider queueing swamped the size effect — this is a tail/makespan cost, not a mean-latency cost. The fix is one line: the cap the operator already thinks is running.
fix: Actually wire the knob. In _build_writer_prompt (abstractive_writer.py:569), truncate the span at the cap the launchers already set: `cap = int(os.environ.get('PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP', '8000') or 8000); quote = quote[:cap]`. NOTE: the provenance token [#ev:eid:start-end] is derived from _member_global_span independently, so truncating the DISPLAYED span without shrinking the token would desync the region gate — safest is to trim the member (the _member_surviving_units_or_drop envelope path at :740 already does exactly this kind of re-anchoring via dataclasses.replace + a re-derived span) so token and text stay consistent. Better still, cap at the sentence-unit level so the fat tail collapses without losing the claim-bearing text.

title: Every NLI verdict funnels through a process-global BoundedSemaphore(4) — the verify stage is hard-capped at ~0.9 verdicts/sec no matter what writer concurrency is set to
mechanism: DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4 builds a PROCESS-GLOBAL threading.BoundedSemaphore(4) (judge_concurrency.py:55,137-145). entailment_judge._post_with_total_deadline wraps the ENTIRE judge POST in `with _acquire_judge_slot():` (entailment_judge.py:155). Confirmed call chain: judge() :740 -> _judge_uncached() :765 -> _post_with_total_deadline() :999 -> :155 semaphore. Commit 0615bc5 moved this blocking POST off the event loop into asyncio.to_thread — so now up to 24 writer threads (PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12 x PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2) run concurrently and ALL of them block on this one 4-slot semaphore. NO launcher (launch_compose_gear_iter1/3/4/5.sh) ever sets PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY, so every real run used the default 4. The semaphore was added for a DIFFERENT workload (the per-claim credibility burst that 429-stormed friendli); the compose writer-verify path inherited it silently. Result: the 48-way parallelism the operator asked for is 4-way at the verify stage.
fix: Set PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY in the compose launchers to match the writer fan-out (e.g. 24-32; glm-5.2 has 27 healthy endpoints per the live OpenRouter /endpoints read, so the 4-host rationale in the comment at judge_concurrency.py:51-54 is stale for this model). Better: make the cap phase-aware — judge_concurrency.py already has a PHASE OVERRIDE mechanism (_CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE, :61) used by the credibility pass; have the abstractive-writer pre-pass install its own higher override for the duration of the pre-pass, so the de-storm protection stays tight for the per-claim burst it was designed for and does not throttle compose.

title: Phase-2 section assembly is a SYNCHRONOUS sequential per-basket loop -> NLI judge runs at concurrency 1 for 92% of wall-clock
mechanism: After the concurrent abstractive prepass (phase-1) finishes, _run_section calls the sync fn _compose_section_per_basket which does `for basket in section_baskets:` and, per basket, composes+verifies each sentence via verify_fn (the NLI entailment judge, an OpenRouter LLM POST) plus bounded redraft repairs, strictly one basket and one sentence at a time. Nothing fans this out. The prepass concurrency knobs (PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12, PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16) do NOT apply to this phase, so the missing-await fix bought nothing here.
fix: Fan out the per-basket verify/redraft in _compose_section_per_basket concurrently (asyncio.gather over baskets, or run each basket's verify via a bounded pool), so multiple NLI judge POSTs are in flight at once instead of 1.

title: _compose_section_per_basket runs synchronously ON the event-loop thread (no await / no to_thread) -> blocks the loop for the entire section, defeating PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS
mechanism: async def _run_section calls the blocking sync _compose_section_per_basket with NO await and NO asyncio.to_thread (code comment at multi_section_generator.py:4938 even states it 'runs synchronously inside the already-running section event loop'). Each internal NLI judge POST blocks on concurrent.futures fut.result(timeout=...) on the loop thread. So while section N is in phase-2, the event loop cannot advance any other coroutine — including a second section's phase-2. PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 therefore cannot overlap section bodies; on a 4-section/346-basket run all section assemblies serialize end-to-end.
fix: Wrap the phase-2 call in asyncio.to_thread (like the prepass fix did for _draft_passes_wrapper) so sections' phase-2 work runs off-loop and two sections can genuinely overlap; combined with Finding 1's intra-section fan-out.

title: NLI/entailment judge path is capped at 4 concurrent (PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY default=4) and is NOT gated by the writer's global Semaphore(16) — the ceiling once serialization is fixed
mechanism: Every entailment POST goes through acquire_judge_slot() -> a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore(4). The writer async calls use a SEPARATE asyncio.Semaphore(PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16) in providers/llm_provider.py and never touch the judge slot. So the two OpenRouter paths have independent, unequal caps. Today the 4-slot cap is moot (serial caller only ever holds 1), but it becomes the binding limit the moment Findings 1/2 are fixed to fan out verification — verification would parallelize to 4, not 12/16.
fix: When enabling concurrent phase-2 verification, also raise PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY to match the intended writer concurrency (e.g. 12-16), and consider unifying the judge under the same global LLM semaphore for one coherent account-wide cap.
